mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in PAYEMS signals expanding payroll income and stronger household cash-flow expectations, which raises expected corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value for 2007-09 was 138265 thousand
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth does not produce higher average 20-day SPX returns than lower PAYEMS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS crossing above 4.5% marks restrictive short-rate policy that raises discount rates and tightens credit conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% from a prior monthly value at or below 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS crossings are followed by 20-day SPX returns that are not lower than unconditional 20-day returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 65% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and rise in UNRATE signals that housing weakness is spilling into labor income, reducing expected consumption and earnings; this produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% year over year while UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value for 2007-08 was 1331 thousand units
fact:UNRATE vintage value for 2007-09 was 4.7 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-down and UNRATE-up trigger does not lower subsequent 60-day SPX returns relative to cases where only one condition is present, or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in RSAFS indicates resilient nominal consumer spending and near-term revenue support for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value for 2007-09 was 380230 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is not associated with higher 20-day SPX returns than negative RSAFS momentum, or if the estimated coefficient on RSAFS momentum is negative in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month change in INDPRO signals weakening real output and operating leverage pressure, which lowers expected earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month contractions are followed by 60-day SPX returns that are not below unconditional returns, or if the signal's hit rate for negative returns is below 40% across walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS combined with non-accelerating CPIAUCSL signals policy easing without an inflation shock, lowering discount rates and supporting valuations; this produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls over 3 months while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation does not rise by more than 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the joint easing and contained-inflation condition does not outperform unconditional 60-day SPX returns, or if FEDFUNDS declines have no stronger effect when CPIAUCSL inflation is contained.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE increase greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value for 2007-09 was 4.7 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are not followed by lower 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered observations, or if triggered observations produce positive returns at or above the unconditional positive rate.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and HOUST is not contracting, broad real demand is expanding without a housing drag, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth is above 0% and HOUST 3-month change is above 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value for 2007-08 was 1331 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive GDPC1 and positive HOUST condition does not generate higher 5-day SPX returns than unconditional returns, or if either component alone fully explains the effect with no interaction gain.
